classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: Chapter XIV. 25–27.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the preceding lesson of the holy Gospel, which is followed by the one that has just been read, the Lord Jesus had said that He and the Father would come to those who loved Them, and make Their abode with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He had also already said above of the Holy Spirit, “But ye shall know Him; for He shall dwell with you, and shall be in you” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: 17): by which we understood that the divine Trinity dwelleth together in the saints as in His own temple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He saith, “These things have I spoken unto you while [still] dwelling with you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That dwelling, therefore, which He promised in the future, is of one kind; and this, which He declares to be present, is of another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one is spiritual, and is realized inwardly by the mind; the other is corporal, and is exhibited outwardly to the eye and the ear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one brings eternal blessedness to those who have been delivered, the other pays its visits in time to those who await deliverance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As regards the one, the Lord never withdraws from those who love Him; as regards the other, He comes and goes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These things, He says, “have I spoken unto you, while [still] dwelling with you;” that is, in His bodily presence, wherein He was visibly conversing with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But the Comfort,” He adds, “[which is] the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, He shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it, then, that the Son speaks, and the Holy Spirit teaches, so that we merely get hold of the words that are uttered by the Son, and then understand them by the teaching of the Spirit as if the Son could speak without the Holy Spirit, or the Holy Spirit teach without the Son: or is it not rather that the Son also teacheth and the Spirit speaketh, and, when it is God that speaketh and teacheth anything, that the Trinity itself is speaking and teaching?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just because it is a Trinity, its persons required to be introduced individually, so that we might hear it in its distinct personality, and understand its inseparable nature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Father speaking in the passage where thou readest, “The Lord said unto me, Thou art my Son:” listen to Him also teaching, in that where thou readest, “Every man that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son, on the other hand, thou hast just heard speaking; for He saith of Himself, “Whatsoever I have said unto you:” and if thou wouldst also know Him as a Teacher, bethink thyself of the Master, when He saith, “One is your Master, even Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Furthermore, of the Holy Spirit, whom thou hast just been told of as a Teacher in the words, “He shall teach you all things,” listen to Him also speaking, where thou readest in the Acts of the Apostles, that the Holy Spirit said to the blessed Peter, “Go with them, for I have sent them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The whole Trinity, therefore, both speaketh and teacheth: but were it not also brought before us in its individual personality, it would certainly altogether surpass the power of human weakness to comprehend it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as it is altogether inseparable in itself, it could never be known as the Trinity, were it always spoken of inseparably; for when we speak of the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit, we certainly do not pronounce them simultaneously, and yet in themselves they cannot be else than simultaneous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He added, “He will bring to your remembrance,” we ought also to understand that we are commanded not to forget that these pre-eminently salutary admonitions are part of that grace which the Holy Spirit brings to our remembrance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Peace,” He said, “I leave with you, my peace I give unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is here we read in the prophet, “Peace upon peace:” peace He leaves with us when going away, His own peace He will give us when He cometh in the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peace He leaveth with us in this world, His own peace He will give us in the world to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His own peace He leaveth with us, and abiding therein we conquer the enemy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His own peace He will give us when, with no more enemies to fight, we shall reign as kings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peace He leaveth with us, that here also we may love one another: His own peace will He give us, where we shall be beyond the possibility of dissension.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peace He leaveth with us, that we may not judge one another of what is secret to each, while here on earth: His own peace will He give us, when He “will make manifest the counsels of the heart; and then shall every man have praise of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet in Him and from Him it is that we have peace, whether that which He leaveth with us when going to the Father, or that which He will give us when we ourselves are brought by Him to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is it He leaveth with us, when ascending from us, save His own presence, which He never withdraweth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He Himself is our peace who hath made both one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is He, therefore, that becomes our peace, both when we believe that He is, and when we see Him as He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, so long as we are in this corruptible body that burdens the soul, and are walking by faith, not by sight, He forsaketh not those who are sojourning at a distance from Himself; how much more, when we have attained to that sight, shall He fill us with Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why is it that, when He said, “Peace I leave with you,” He did not add, “my;” but when He said, “I give unto you,” He there made use of it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is “my” to be understood even where it is not expressed, on the ground that what is expressed once may have a reference to both?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or may it not be that here also we have some underlying truth that has to be asked and sought for, and opened up to those who knock thereat?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what, if by His own peace He meant such to be understood as that which He possesses Himself? whereas the peace, which He leaves us in this world, may more properly be termed our peace than His.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He, who is altogether without sin, has no elements of discord in Himself; while the peace we possess, meanwhile, is such that in the midst of it we have still to be saying, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain kind of peace, accordingly, we do possess, inasmuch as we delight in the law of God after the inward man: but it is not a full peace, for we see another law in our members warring against the law of our mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same way we have peace in our relations with one another, just because, in mutually loving, we have a mutual confidence in one another: but no more is such a peace as that complete, for we see not the thoughts of one another’s hearts; and we have severally better or worse opinions in certain respects of one another than is warranted by the reality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so that peace, although left us by Him, is our peace: for were it not from Him, we should not be possessing it, such as it is; but such is not the peace He has Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if we keep what we received to the end, then such as He has shall we have, when we shall have no elements of discord of our own, and we shall have no secrets hid from one another in our hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I am not ignorant that these words of the Lord may be taken so as to seem only a repetition of the same idea, “Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you:” so that after saying “peace,” He only repeated it in saying “my peace;” and what He had meant in saying “I leave with you,” He simply repeated in saying “I give unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let each one understand it as he pleases; but it is my delight, as I believe it is yours also, my beloved brethren, to keep such hold of that peace here, where our hearts are making common cause against the adversary, that we may be ever longing for the peace which there will be no adversary to disturb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Lord proceeded to say, “Not as the world giveth, give I unto you,” what else does He mean but, Not as those give who love the world, give I unto you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For their aim in giving themselves peace is that, exempt from the annoyance of lawsuits and wars, they may find enjoyment, not in God, but in the friendship of the world; and although they give the righteous peace, in ceasing to persecute them, there can be no true peace where there is no real harmony, because their hearts are at variance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as one is called a consort who unites his lot (sortem) with another, so may he be termed concordant whose heart has entered into a similar union.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, therefore, beloved, with whom Christ leaveth peace, and to whom He giveth His own peace, not after the world’s way, but in a way worthy of Him by whom the world was made, that we should be of one heart with Himself, having our hearts run into one, that this one heart, set on that which is above, may escape the corruption of the earth.
